competitions.title: GENIUS Olympiad 2026
competitions.shortDesc: An international high school competition focused on environmental issues across various disciplines.
competitions.fullDetails: GENIUS Olympiad is an international high school competition hosted in New York, where every project must connect to the environment in some way. Categories include science, engineering, robotics, business, art, short film, music, creative writing, and, new for 2026, AI. Approximately 50% of submissions are accepted as finalists, and roughly 80% of finalists receive some form of medal. The event draws around 1,300 students from over 70 countries each year, with a week of presentations, networking, and optional trips.
competitions.deadline: Typically in early Spring, check website for exact 2026 dates
competitions.location: New York (in-person finals), with regional dates varying.
competitions.prizes: Medals for finalists, with various levels of recognition.
competitions.rounds: Regional competitions leading to national finals.
competitions.fee: $50 application fee, plus $425 if selected for in-person finals.

competitions.title: Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair (ISEF) 2026
competitions.shortDesc: The world's largest pre-collegiate science research competition for high school students.
competitions.fullDetails: The Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair (ISEF) is the world's largest and most prestigious pre-college science research competition, bringing together nearly 2,000 high school students who have won regional competitions from around the world. Participants conduct original research projects and present them to judges. ISEF helps students develop advanced research, critical thinking, and presentation skills, with top projects earning global recognition, scholarships, and significant advantages for college and STEM career pathways. Qualification for ISEF happens through affiliated regional and state-level fairs globally.
competitions.deadline: First round (regional fairs) typically Jan/Feb 2026, check local fair websites for detailed dates.
competitions.prizes: Prizes at the international level are in the tens of thousands, including scholarships and global recognition.
competitions.rounds: Multiple rounds, starting with regional/state fairs leading to the international competition.
competitions.fee: Varies by regional fair; typically free to enter regional events that qualify for ISEF.

competitions.title: 2026 International Youth Competition
competitions.shortDesc: An international competition inviting students to share ideas and perspectives on social challenges through visual art, essays, or open submissions.
competitions.fullDetails: The 2026 International Youth Competition strives for greater interest and participation in social challenges from students around the world. The theme for 2026 is 'Why Me?'. Students can submit entries in Visual Art (drawings, illustrations, paintings, photos), Essay (persuasive or creative written works in any language with English translation), or an Open category for other creative ideas. The competition is open to primary, middle, and high school students, as well as college students, as individuals or teams. Judging is based on message, idea, originality, and delivery.
competitions.interest: visual art, essay writing, social challenges
competitions.eventDate: Winners announced by end of September 2026.
competitions.prizes: Awards are given for each category in each division (Primary, Middle School, High School, College). All participants receive a 'Distinguished Achievement' certificate.

competitions.title: Conrad Challenge
competitions.shortDesc: An international competition challenging high school students to develop innovative and entrepreneurial solutions to global challenges.
competitions.fullDetails: The Conrad Challenge is open to high school students aged 13-18 globally. Participants can form teams of up to five, supervised by a mentor. The competition is free to enter and focuses on fostering entrepreneurship and innovation, with no prior experience required. The 2026-2027 round is expected to open for registrations in August 2026.
competitions.eventDate: Varies by stage, summit typically in April
competitions.location: Online (initial stages), potentially in-person summit for finalists
competitions.prizes: Recognition, mentorship, networking opportunities (specific prizes vary)

competitions.title: NCSS Student Leadership & Research Conference
competitions.shortDesc: A conference for high school students to present STEM research, engage in leadership development, and explore entrepreneurship.
competitions.fullDetails: The 2026 NCSS Student Leadership & Research Conference is hosted by the National Consortium of STEM Schools. It aims to build on student research while expanding its focus to include leadership development and entrepreneurship in STEM. Students can present original research, attend leadership sessions, engage in entrepreneurship programming, and interact with active researchers.
competitions.deadline: To be announced (registration details not yet available, but the event is July 16-18, 2026)
competitions.prizes: Opportunity to present research, networking, learning experiences

competitions.title: International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO)
competitions.shortDesc: The World Championship Mathematics Competition for high school students, held annually.
competitions.fullDetails: The International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO) is the most prestigious mathematics competition for high school students globally, attracting participants from over 100 countries. Students must qualify through their respective country's national mathematics Olympiad. The competition features challenging problem-solving rounds.
competitions.deadline: Varies by national qualification pathway (July 2026 for international finals)
competitions.location: To be announced (host country varies annually)
competitions.prizes: Gold, Silver, and Bronze medals, international recognition
competitions.rounds: Multiple (national qualification leading to international finals)
competitions.fee: Free Entry (qualification may have local fees)

competitions.title: Google Science Fair
competitions.shortDesc: A globally recognized online science competition inviting students to solve real-world problems through original research or prototypes.
competitions.fullDetails: The Google Science Fair is open to students aged 13-18 from all countries. Participants identify a real-world problem and design an original research project or prototype to address it, submitting their findings via an online platform. Judges include scientists, engineers, and Google researchers.
competitions.eventDate: To be announced (virtual submission and judging)
competitions.prizes: Scholarships, experiences, recognition (specifics vary annually)
competitions.link: https://www.googlesciencefair.com/ (Note: As of May 2026, the specific 2026 page was not live, but mentioned in 2026 competition lists.)

competitions.title: International Economics Olympiad (IEO)
competitions.shortDesc: An international competition designed to challenge and inspire high school students in economics, financial literacy, and business.
competitions.fullDetails: The International Economics Olympiad is a prestigious competition for high school students, focusing on economics, financial literacy, and business. Participants typically qualify through national-level competitions. The international event brings together top students to solve complex economic problems and business cases.
competitions.deadline: Varies by national qualification pathway (August 2026 for international finals)
competitions.location: To be announced (host country varies annually)
competitions.prizes: Medals, international recognition
competitions.rounds: Multiple (national qualification leading to international finals)
competitions.fee: Free Entry (qualification may have local fees)

competitions.title: International Sustainability Competition
competitions.shortDesc: Challenges high school students to develop original solutions to pressing environmental and sustainability issues.
competitions.fullDetails: This competition invites high school students worldwide to identify a sustainability challenge (e.g., climate, water, food systems, biodiversity, clean energy) and develop an original research-based or innovation-based solution. Submissions include a written report and presentation, evaluated by international judges from academia and industry. The competition is virtual for submissions.
competitions.deadline: To be announced (virtual submission period typically allows for several months)
competitions.prizes: Recognition, exposure to international judges
competitions.link: Not explicitly found, but mentioned as a top international competition in LogoLife Blog

competitions.title: 2026 Global Essay Prize (John Locke Institute)
competitions.shortDesc: A prestigious essay competition encouraging critical thinking across various humanities and social science subjects for students up to 18 years old.
competitions.fullDetails: The John Locke Institute's Global Essay Prize is open to students up to 18 years old worldwide. It challenges participants to write compelling essays on thought-provoking questions across categories like Philosophy, Politics, Economics, History, Psychology, Theology, and Law. It is an online competition with free entry. The deadline for 2026 submissions is May 31, 2026.
competitions.interest: humanities, social sciences, critical thinking, writing
competitions.prizes: Recognition, prestige for college applications

competitions.title: National STEM Festival
competitions.shortDesc: A U.S. competition where students address real-world problems aligned with six challenge themes, with top projects earning a trip to Washington D.C.
competitions.fullDetails: The National STEM Festival is open to U.S. students in grades 7-12. Participants create projects that address real-world problems within themes like Aerospace Innovation, Environmental Stewardship, Future Food, Health & Medicine, Power the Planet, and Tech for Good. Top projects from each U.S. state, D.C., and territories are selected as Challenge Finalists and submit a video for the second round. National Champions are awarded a trip to Washington D.C. in 2026.
competitions.prizes: Trip to Washington D.C., national recognition
competitions.rounds: Multiple (project submission, video round, national champion selection)

competitions.title: Global Impact Competition 2026
competitions.shortDesc: Challenges students worldwide to investigate community issues through field research and policy writing to kickstart meaningful passion projects.
competitions.fullDetails: The Global Impact Competition 2026 calls on high school students from around the world to conduct field research on a community issue and develop policy writing solutions. It's designed for students looking to engage in impactful projects and connect with outstanding peers globally. Potential internship opportunities are available for top achievers.
competitions.prizes: Up to $250 cash prize, potential internship opportunities
competitions.rounds: Multiple stages (field research, policy writing, evaluation)

competitions.title: Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge (GQMC)
competitions.shortDesc: An international, accessible, research-oriented challenge for high school and university students interested in the quantum world.
competitions.fullDetails: The Global Quantum Mechanics Challenge (GQMC) is an international competition aimed at high school and university students interested in quantum mechanics. It provides an accessible and research-oriented platform for learning about the quantum world. Top achievers in the final round receive cash prizes.
competitions.prizes: 1st Prize Senior: 250 USD, 2nd Prize Senior: 175 USD
competitions.fee: To be announced (initial image submission free)

competitions.title: 67th International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO) 2026
competitions.shortDesc: The world's most prestigious mathematics competition for high school students, featuring challenging proof-based problems.
competitions.fullDetails: The IMO is an annual global competition designed for high school students with a strong interest in problem-solving and logical thinking. Students are selected through rigorous national-level processes in their respective countries. The competition evaluates mathematical creativity and precision, and encourages international collaboration among young mathematicians.
competitions.deadline: Final student details submission by April 29, 2026; full payment of fees by May 29, 2026.
competitions.prizes: Gold, Silver, and Bronze medals, national recognition.
competitions.rounds: National selection process, followed by the international competition.
competitions.fee: Free to participate after national qualification (participation fees covered by national bodies).

competitions.title: Zero Robotics 2026 High School Tournament: Galactic Greenhouse
competitions.shortDesc: A unique challenge where high school students program NASA's Astrobee robots onboard the International Space Station (ISS).
competitions.fullDetails: Zero Robotics is an educational program that hosts tournaments to teach middle and high school students to write code and program with robots. The 2026 High School Tournament, themed 'Galactic Greenhouse,' tasks students with programming NASA's Astrobee robots to manage crops in microgravity conditions on the ISS. This program offers an unparalleled opportunity to engage with space robotics and advanced coding in a real-world space environment.
competitions.deadline: Registration is open for the 2026 tournament.
competitions.eventDate: 2026 (specific tournament dates not yet detailed, but the program runs in 2026).
competitions.location: Online programming; robots operate on the International Space Station (ISS).
competitions.prizes: Educational experience, recognition for successful programs, engagement with NASA technology.
competitions.fee: Not specified (educational programs can be free or have a fee).

competitions.title: IEEE Computer Society Global Student Challenge (GSC) 2026
competitions.shortDesc: An international challenge for students to develop innovative solutions to programming and data analytic problems.
competitions.fullDetails: The IEEE Computer Society Global Student Challenge 2026 invites student teams to create innovative solutions for real-world programming and data analytic problems. Teams of two work on challenge prompts related to areas like Backdoor Attacks in Distributed ML, Vulnerabilities in Github CI/CD Workflows, and Analyzing Resource Usage in Data Centers. The competition involves virtual submission for Phase I and in-person presentations for finalists in Phase II.
competitions.eventDate: Phase I: July 13 - August 9, 2026 (Virtual); Phase II (Finals): December 11-14, 2026
competitions.location: Phase I: Online; Phase II: Los Angeles, California, USA
competitions.prizes: Total prize pool of US$5,000 among top 5 teams, plus 1 year of IEEE and IEEE Computer Society Student Membership.

competitions.title: Global Challenge Weekend
competitions.shortDesc: An intensive online weekend experience for high school students to address global challenges aligned with UN Sustainable Development Goals.
competitions.fullDetails: High school students from across the world meet online to brainstorm possibilities, research options, pitch ideas, develop prototypes, and present their projects. Teams of 3-5 students collaborate over 36 hours with faculty mentors available for guidance. Topics bridge STEM and humanities.
competitions.interest: STEM, Humanities, UN SDGs, Innovation, Problem-Solving, Design Thinking
competitions.deadline: Registration Open (check official website for final deadline)
competitions.prizes: Awards for 'Best Global Challenge Solution'.
competitions.fee: Not specified, check official website for details.

competitions.title: Oxford Saïd Global Climate Tech Challenge
competitions.shortDesc: High school students and teachers worldwide develop tech-driven solutions to the climate crisis.
competitions.fullDetails: This free-to-enter competition invites participants to use technology, creativity, and problem-solving skills to generate solutions and lesson plans for global or local climate challenges, such as air pollution, extreme weather, food security, and water scarcity. Shortlisted student teams and their teachers receive an all-expenses-paid trip to Oxford in August.
competitions.interest: Climate Change, Technology, Innovation, Environmental Science, Entrepreneurship
competitions.deadline: Not specified, check official site for 2026 deadlines.
competitions.prizes: Full scholarship to the Future Climate Tech Innovators summer school for the winning student team and a place on an Oxford Executive Education program for the winning teacher.

competitions.title: regeneron science talent search 2026
competitions.shortDesc: the nation's oldest and most prestigious science and math competition for high school seniors, recognizing groundbreaking research.
competitions.fullDetails: this competition identifies, inspires, and engages promising future leaders in stem. students submit original research projects in various scientific fields. the top 40 finalists compete for over $1.8 million in awards, with a top prize of $250,000. scholars and their schools receive $2,000 each. it is open to high school seniors (typically 16-18 years old), and generally for grades 9-12.
competitions.prizes: over $1.8 million in awards, top prize $250,000, $2,000 for scholars and their schools.
competitions.rounds: multiple rounds (application, scholars, finalists, grand awards)
competitions.fee: not specified as having a direct fee for application

competitions.title: international young physicists' tournament (iypt) 2026
competitions.shortDesc: a scientific competition for teams of secondary school students, dubbed the 'physics world cup'.
competitions.fullDetails: teams work on 17 open-ended inquiry problems for almost a year, designing and performing experiments. the competition involves 'physics fights' where participants act as reporters, opponents, and reviewers, judged by expert physicists. it is open to secondary school students.
competitions.deadline: problems published late july (prior year). team/leader registration by may 25, 2026.
competitions.prizes: medals awarded.

competitions.title: global youth impact summit 2026

competitions.interest: leadership, social change, sustainable development, innovation
competitions.prizes: fully funded seats cover airfare, accommodation, meals, materials, visa support, certification, networking, speaking opportunities.

competitions.title: tony robbins global youth leadership summit (gyls) 2026
competitions.shortDesc: a leadership summit for young people focused on personal growth, service learning, and leadership skills.
competitions.fullDetails: the summit includes small group discussions, hands-on service learning, leadership simulation games, and keynote speakers. participants identify personal goals and commit to community service. open to ambassadors (14-16 years old), alumni (15-17 years old), and youth leaders (16-17 years old, invitation-only).

competitions.title: international olympiad in informatics (ioi) 2026
competitions.shortDesc: a prestigious annual competition for secondary school students worldwide, focusing on programming and problem-solving skills.
competitions.fullDetails: the international olympiad in informatics (ioi) is one of five global science olympiads, aiming to encourage interest in informatics (computer science) and information technology. the 38th edition, ioi 2026, will be hosted by uzbekistan from august 9 to 16, 2026. participants, who must be enrolled in school and not older than 20 years on july 1st of the competition year, typically qualify through national or regional contests. the competition consists of two stages where students solve and program algorithmic problems individually. each country can send a team of up to four students. the official programming language for ioi 2026 is exclusively c/c++.
competitions.prizes: medals, international recognition
competitions.rounds: national selection, then international competition (two stages)

competitions.title: international computer science competition (icsc) 2026
competitions.shortDesc: a global online computer science competition for middle, high school, and university students testing logical thinking, analytical skills, and coding.
competitions.fullDetails: the international computer science competition (icsc) 2026 is designed to test students' logical thinking, analytical skills, coding ability, and understanding of computer science concepts beyond the regular classroom curriculum. it is conducted in multiple online rounds, primarily for individual participation. eligible students from all countries are divided into junior (<16/grade 10), youth (<19/grades 11-13), and senior (19+/university) categories. programming solutions can be written in python, java, c, or c++. the qualification round submission deadline is july 5, 2026, with a pre-final round registration deadline on august 11, 2026, and the final exam on september 15, 2026. the competition offers cash prizes, certificates, and national awards.
competitions.prizes: up to $1,500 total cash prizes, certificates, national awards
competitions.fee: free for qualification round (details for later rounds may vary)

competitions.title: international english language olympiad (ielo) 2026

competitions.title: genes in space competition
competitions.shortDesc: a competition for grade 7 to grade 12 students in the united states to design dna experiments for space.
competitions.fullDetails: students can participate individually or as part of a team of up to two. no prior experience in conducting space-related research is required. finalists are announced in may and receive mentoring, with winners announced in july, who then participate in a space biology camp in the fall.
competitions.prizes: mentoring, space biology camp, recognition.

competitions.title: physicsbowl
competitions.shortDesc: an international physics competition for high school students, testing knowledge from introductory to advanced levels.
competitions.fullDetails: students typically participate as a team representing their school or physics club, although individual participation is also allowed. the exam is usually conducted at their respective schools or designated testing centers.
competitions.prizes: not specified, typically recognition and certificates

competitions.title: international olympiad in informatics (ioi)
competitions.shortDesc: a prestigious annual competitive programming competition for secondary school students worldwide.
competitions.fullDetails: each country sends a team of up to four students, selected through national computing contests. students compete individually to solve six complicated algorithmic tasks by writing computer programs in c++ over two days. the top 50% of contestants are awarded medals.
competitions.prizes: gold, silver, bronze medals.
competitions.rounds: national selection, international final (2 competition days)
competitions.fee: not specified, likely covered by national organizers for qualified teams

competitions.title: world robot olympiad (wro) international final
competitions.shortDesc: a global robotics competition where youths design and program robots to solve real-world challenges.
competitions.fullDetails: the 2026 theme is 'robots meet culture,' exploring how robots can shape, protect, and grow art and culture. teams qualify through national wro competitions. the event nurtures innovation, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills in youth.
competitions.prizes: not explicitly mentioned, but typically includes awards and recognition
competitions.fee: not specified, likely varies by national organizer

competitions.title: international informatics olympiad in teams (iiot)
competitions.shortDesc: a computer programming competition for teams of four high school students, focusing on algorithmic thinking and teamwork.
competitions.fullDetails: the competition brings together talented students, coordinating teachers, and it specialists from several countries, fostering collaboration, creativity, algorithmic thinking, and team spirit.
competitions.deadline: not specified, national selection likely applies
competitions.prizes: not specified, but recognition and experience

competitions.title: international mathematical olympiad (imo)
competitions.shortDesc: the world championship mathematics competition for high school students, widely regarded as the most prestigious.
competitions.fullDetails: held annually with over 100 countries participating, each sending a team of up to six students. students compete individually on six extremely difficult problems across geometry, number theory, algebra, and combinatorics over two days. calculators are banned, and the top 50% of contestants receive awards.
competitions.prizes: gold, silver, bronze medals.
competitions.rounds: national selection, international final (2 competition days)
competitions.fee: not specified, likely covered by national organizers for qualified teams

competitions.title: regeneron science talent search (sts) 2027 application
competitions.shortDesc: a prestigious science competition for high school seniors in the us, focusing on identifying future stem leaders through original research.
competitions.fullDetails: open to u.s. citizens or permanent residents who are high school seniors. students submit an original research project in science, math, engineering, or medicine. $3.1 million in awards are distributed annually, with a $250,000 top prize. the application for sts 2027 opens in 2026.
competitions.eventDate: finalists typically announced in january 2027, competition in march 2027
competitions.prizes: $3.1 million total awards, $250,000 first prize.
competitions.rounds: application, scholar selection (top 300), finalist selection (top 40), final competition

competitions.title: plural+ youth video festival
competitions.shortDesc: a un-backed video festival inviting young filmmakers worldwide under the age of 25 to submit short films addressing pressing social issues.
competitions.fullDetails: the festival highlights the power of youth as agents of social change. it accepts short films in three age categories.
competitions.deadline: 2026-xx-xx (specific date for 2026 not found, implied annual)
competitions.prizes: not specified, but recognition through un-backed festival

competitions.title: capture the future: global youth storytelling initiative for people and nature
competitions.shortDesc: an initiative encouraging youth to document local actions, traditions, and innovations for a sustainable future through photography and short-form video.
competitions.fullDetails: the initiative aims to promote sustainable practices and environmental awareness among young people worldwide.
competitions.prizes: not specified

competitions.title: international letter-writing competition for young people
competitions.shortDesc: an annual competition for young people to write letters on a given theme, promoting literacy and global connection.
competitions.fullDetails: the 2026 theme is 'write a letter to a friend about why human connection matters in a digital world.' participants submit letters through postal services.
competitions.prizes: not specified, but recognition and participation certificates

competitions.title: robotics for good youth challenge
competitions.shortDesc: a un-based educational robotics championship where robots provide innovative, practical solutions to real-world problems.
competitions.fullDetails: the competition promotes the use of open-source software and hardware, allowing participants full freedom in assembling and programming their robots to address global challenges.
competitions.prizes: not specified

competitions.title: world youth science knowledge competition
competitions.shortDesc: an online examination event testing science knowledge for young participants worldwide.
competitions.fullDetails: all entries must be completed independently by the participant. the examination is conducted online, and login information is sent via whatsapp or email during the examination period.
competitions.prizes: awards for mentors and groups (implied also for individuals).

competitions.title: build big dreams youth stem conference
competitions.shortDesc: a conference introducing 7th-10th grade attendees to stem careers through hands-on activities, presentations, and networking.
competitions.fullDetails: hosted by the u.s. department of energy's princeton plasma physics laboratory (pppl) at princeton university. attendees spend the day learning about various stem careers and networking with prominent scientists and engineers worldwide.
competitions.prizes: exposure, networking opportunities

studyResources.subject: mathematics, computer science, english
studyResources.title: mit opencourseware for high school
studyResources.description: open educational resources, including course materials from mit, selected to help high school students prepare for ap exams and explore college-level concepts across science, mathematics, engineering, humanities, and social sciences. a portion of the original highlights for high school content has been migrated to the main ocw platform, accessible via searching 'high school' or 'non-credit'.

studyResources.subject: mathematics, computer science, english
studyResources.title: edx free high school courses
studyResources.description: offers numerous free online high school and ap-level courses from top universities in subjects like computer science, calculus, geometry, algebra, and english. students can audit courses for free to prepare for college or augment their high school studies.

studyResources.subject: mathematics, computer science, english
studyResources.title: coursera courses for high school students
studyResources.description: provides a selection of college-level courses, many free, from leading universities. subjects include computer science, data science, english (e.g., 'writing in english at university'), and mathematics. programs like google ai essentials and data analytics are also accessible for free through scholarships or financial aid.

studyResources.subject: mathematics, computer science, english
studyResources.title: free igcse past papers (tutopiya)
studyResources.description: access thousands of free igcse past papers with mark schemes from cambridge and edexcel exam boards for subjects including mathematics (0607, 0580, 0606), english language (0500), and computer science (0478). also includes gcse and a-level papers.

studyResources.subject: mathematics, computer science, english
studyResources.title: ap central free-response questions and scoring information
studyResources.description: the official college board website offers free-response questions, scoring information, and student samples from past ap exams for various subjects, including ap computer science a, ap computer science principles, ap english language and composition, ap english literature and composition, ap calculus ab, and ap calculus bc.

studyResources.subject: computer science
studyResources.title: code.org high school computer science curriculum
studyResources.description: offers free year-long curricula for computer science principles (ap-aligned), computer science a (java), computer science discoveries (web, apps, games), and artificial intelligence resources for grades 3-12.

studyResources.subject: mathematics, computer science, english
studyResources.title: khan academy high school & college courses
studyResources.description: provides a free, world-class education with video lessons, practice questions, and personalized study plans across math (algebra, geometry, precalculus, ap calculus), english language arts, and computing (ap/college computer science principles, intro to python, javascript, computer science theory).

studyResources.subject: computer science
studyResources.title: codehs free k-12 computer science courses
studyResources.description: over 100 free customizable k-12 computer science courses, including high school python, java, cybersecurity, and ap cs. each course includes engaging lessons, projects, video tutorials, and quizzes.

studyResources.subject: mathematics, computer science, english
studyResources.title: ib documents collection of past papers
studyResources.description: provides a collection of ib past papers, question banks, and other helpful resources for ib students across various subjects, including mathematics, computer science, and english.

studyResources.subject: computer science
studyResources.title: cambridge igcse computer science (0478) past papers (papacambridge)
studyResources.description: provides cambridge igcse computer science 0478 latest past papers, specimens, question papers, and marking schemes from 2002 up to the latest session, all available for free download in pdf format.

studyResources.subject: mathematics
studyResources.title: international baccalaureate (ib) maths past exam papers (mymathscloud)
studyResources.description: contains all international baccalaureate (ib) maths past exam papers for analysis and approaches (standard and higher level) and applications and interpretation (standard and higher level).

studyResources.subject: mathematics
studyResources.title: cambridge international as & a level mathematics (9709) past papers
studyResources.description: a comprehensive collection of cambridge international a level mathematics past papers (9709), including question papers and mark schemes for all variants. these are essential for international exam preparation and cover years from 2002 to the latest sessions. from march 2026, new accessible layouts will be introduced, but content remains unchanged.

studyResources.subject: computer science
studyResources.title: ib computer science consolidated revision guide (hl)
studyResources.description: a consolidated revision guide covering key concepts in ib computer science for higher level students. topics include systems planning, implementation, design, cpu architecture, memory types, operating systems, application software, data representation, and network architectures. it provides a comprehensive overview for exam preparation.

studyResources.subject: computer science
studyResources.title: code.org computer science principles curriculum
studyResources.description: a free, year-long curriculum introducing students to foundational concepts of computer science and challenging them to explore how computing and technology can impact the world. it can be taught as an introductory class or an ap course, covering concepts in ai, algorithms, and problem-solving.

studyResources.subject: mathematics
studyResources.title: openstax high school mathematics textbooks
studyResources.description: openstax provides free pdf versions of high school mathematics textbooks, including algebra, trigonometry, and statistics. these resources are designed for various high school-level math classes and are available for download, with teacher resources also accessible upon verification.

studyResources.subject: english
studyResources.title: oer commons high school english language arts collection
studyResources.description: a collection of open educational resources for english language arts, including lesson plans, writing prompts, discussion topics, and interactive activities. it covers areas like reading informational text, literature, grammar, and vocabulary, offering materials for both teachers and students.

studyResources.subject: english
studyResources.title: berkeleyx: ap english literature & composition - parts 1 & 2
studyResources.description: two free online courses, 'stories' and 'poems,' focusing on reading, interpreting, and writing about fiction, gothic literature, and various poetry genres. the curriculum helps broaden vocabulary, sharpen academic and creative writing skills, and prepares students for the ap exam and further literary study.

studyResources.subject: mathematics
studyResources.title: cambridge igcse mathematics (0580) syllabus for 2025, 2026 and 2027
studyResources.description: official syllabus document outlining essential subject content for cambridge igcse mathematics. covers number theory, algebra, graphing, geometry, mensuration, coordinate geometry, trigonometry, transformation, vectors, probability, and statistics. tiered to allow all candidates to achieve and progress.
studyResources.link: https://www.cambridgeinternational.org/images/663851-2025-2027-syllabus.pdf (direct pdf link, check cambridge international website for most current year if link breaks)

studyResources.subject: mathematics
studyResources.title: cambridge igcse additional mathematics (0606) syllabus for 2025, 2026 and 2027
studyResources.description: official syllabus for cambridge igcse additional mathematics, designed for students with a strong aptitude. deepens understanding of algebra, geometry, calculus, and trigonometry, emphasizing mathematical rigor and problem-solving. provides a strong foundation for further study like ibdp mathematics.
studyResources.link: https://www.cambridgeinternational.org/images/663849-2025-2027-syllabus.pdf (direct pdf link, check cambridge international website for most current year if link breaks)

studyResources.subject: computer science
studyResources.title: ib diploma programme computer science guide (first assessment 2027)
studyResources.description: this official guide outlines the ib computer science course, focusing on computational thinking and problem-solving. it supports programming in both java and python and covers system fundamentals, computer organization, networks, databases, ai, cybersecurity, and emerging technologies. it also details the internal assessment structure.

studyResources.subject: computer science
studyResources.title: free ib computer science (sl) resources - knowt ib hub
studyResources.description: a collection of free study guides, flashcards, and resources for ib computer science standard level. covers system fundamentals, computer organization, networks, computational thinking, problem-solving and programming, databases, modeling and simulation, web science, and object-oriented programming.

studyResources.description: free online study materials for cbse class 10 mathematics, including solutions, question papers, revision notes, syllabus, and sample papers. covers real numbers, polynomials, linear equations, quadratic equations, arithmetic progressions, triangles, coordinate geometry, trigonometry, and circles.

studyResources.subject: mathematics

studyResources.description: chapter-wise notes for cbse class 10 mathematics, simplifying complex concepts with diagrams, flowcharts, derivations, equations, and formulas. ideal for comprehensive exam preparation and quick revision.

studyResources.description: the curriculum allows units to be taught independently or as a full-year course, focusing on fun, hands-on projects and creative problem-solving. 'ai discoveries,' a new middle school course built around ai literacy, is available for the 2026–27 school year.

dailyInspiration.title: the self-taught prodigy: ahaan rungta's path to mit at 15
dailyInspiration.story: ahaan rungta's educational journey began exceptionally early, being homeschooled primarily through mit's free open-courseware (ocw) from the tender age of five. his parents, after moving to florida from india, discovered ocw the same year mit launched its initiative to publish all course materials online for free. ahaan, an avid reader from a young age, essentially acquired his entire elementary and secondary education through these online resources and mitx. by age twelve, his family moved to massachusetts to be closer to mit, a testament to his focused ambition. for his thirteenth birthday, his sole wish was a visit to the institute. his self-directed learning approach saw him tackle linear algebra by watching professor gil strang's video lectures. at just 15, ahaan rungta was admitted to the mit class of 2019, a remarkable achievement that he humbly attributed to the 'gift' of ocw. he continued to use these online resources to supplement his on-campus undergraduate experience, highlighting a lifelong commitment to learning and a deep appreciation for accessible education.
dailyInspiration.takeaway: passionately pursuing knowledge through available resources can open extraordinary doors to even the most elite institutions, regardless of conventional pathways.
